summary: AI is entering the Agentic Era — autonomous systems that plan, use tools, and self-correct. Test-time compute makes small models reason like large ones. The next wave of startups will be built on agent infrastructure, vertical AI workers, and on-device intelligence.

trends.signal: Anthropic Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Manus, Devin — autonomous coding agents shipping to production. Multi-agent frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen) maturing.
trends.learn: Agent architectures, tool-use protocols (MCP, A2A), planning algorithms, multi-agent coordination, prompt engineering for tool calls
trends.build: Vertical AI workers for regulated industries (legal, healthcare, finance). Agent evaluation platforms. Human-in-the-loop oversight tools. Agent-to-agent communication standards.
papers: TTI framework: 12B model beats larger agents via test-time interaction
SWEnergy: energy efficiency in agentic frameworks

trends.signal: Test-time compute scaling — giving models 'thinking time' dramatically improves results. DiffCoT, Buffer of Thoughts, hierarchical reasoning. RL for reasoning (DeepSeek-R1, o1/o3).
trends.learn: Chain-of-thought variants, test-time compute strategies, verifier models, reward modeling, constitutional AI alignment
trends.build: Reasoning-as-a-service APIs. Domain-specific reasoning models (math, code, science). Verifier-as-a-service. Reasoning model fine-tuning platforms.
papers: The Art of Scaling Test-Time Compute (30B tokens across 8 models)
Does RL Really Incentivize Reasoning Capacity?

trends.signal: 2026: shift from LLMs to SLMs. Gemma, Phi, Llama-mini. On-device AI. Quantization advances (1-bit, 2-bit). Mixture of Experts pruning.
trends.learn: Model compression, quantization (GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF), distillation, speculative decoding, edge deployment (CoreML, ONNX, MediaPipe)
trends.build: On-device AI SDKs. SLM fine-tuning platforms. Edge inference optimization tools. Domain-specific tiny models for IoT/sensors.
papers: Dell: 'Smaller AI Models Take Over' — 2026 prediction
Efficient Expert Pruning for MoE LLMs
1.5B reasoning model outranks 13B LLMs as discriminator

trends.signal: Vision-language models becoming default. Video understanding. Audio+text+vision fusion. 3D scene generation. JanusMesh for 3D illusions.
trends.learn: Vision transformers, CLIP-style contrastive learning, video understanding architectures, audio processing, cross-modal attention
trends.build: Video understanding APIs. Multimodal search. AR/VR AI assistants. Real-time video analytics. Multimodal content moderation.
papers: MemoryWAM: world action modeling with persistent memory

trends.signal: Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code — AI writes production code. SWE-bench scores climbing. Agentic coding with full repo context becoming standard.
trends.learn: Code LLM architectures, repository-level understanding, program synthesis, test generation, code review automation, AST manipulation
trends.build: Vertical coding agents (security audit, refactoring, migration). Codebase knowledge graphs. AI-native IDEs. Automated dependency management.
papers: SWE-bench verified: coding benchmarks driving agent development

trends.signal: MCP (Model Context Protocol), A2A (Agent-to-Agent). Every model needs tools, memory, and orchestration. The 'picks and shovels' of the agent gold rush.
trends.learn: MCP/A2A protocols, vector databases, agent memory architectures, observability and tracing (OpenTelemetry for agents), sandboxing and security
trends.build: Agent hosting platforms. Tool registry/marketplace. Agent monitoring and observability. Memory-as-a-service. Agent security and guardrail platforms.
papers: MCP specification becoming de facto standard

startup_thesis.wave_1_2023_2025: Foundation models and chatbots — OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere captured this
startup_thesis.wave_2_2025_now: Agentic AI and vertical applications — the current land grab. Winners will own specific domains, not general chat.
startup_thesis.wave_3_2026_2028: Physical AI + agent ecosystems. Agents that act in the real world (robotics, vehicles, manufacturing). Agent-to-agent economies. On-device intelligence everywhere.
startup_thesis.where_to_play: The biggest opportunity for a solo/small-team founder is in the 'agent infrastructure' layer — tools, protocols, and platforms that every AI agent needs — or in a deep vertical where you have domain expertise that AI generalists don't.
